required_contains.text: ## How a Delay Defends, and When It Does Not
The configured delay on a proxy is the main structural defense, but only under conditions. The
guidance explains that a non-zero delay forces the delegate to announce a call and wait a number of
blocks before it can execute, which gives the protected account a window to reject it. With a zero
delay there is no such window, so a leaked delegate key can act as fast as the chain accepts the
transaction. The catch is that the window only helps if the owner actually watches for pending
